skill.name: chatcrystal-task-writeback
skill.description: Write reusable ChatCrystal task memories after substantive work completes. Use when implementation or debugging produced a durable fix, pitfall, pattern, or decision worth preserving, and when the environment can either persist it through `write_task_memory` or emit a structured memory candidate for later save.
skill.summary: Persist durable task outcomes into ChatCrystal memory after meaningful implementation or debugging work.
skill.author: ZengLiangYi

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-crypto detections are false positives caused by Markdown inline-code formatting and descriptive text. The real risk is intentional persistence of task metadata to ChatCrystal memory, which is publishable with clear user awareness.

medium_findings.title: Task Memory Writeback Persists Project Metadata
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the runtime to call write_task_memory with task fields such as project_dir, cwd, branch, files_touched, error_signatures, and source_agent. This is the intended function, but it can persist sensitive project context if users apply it to confidential work.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited instructions explicitly name metadata fields that may be saved or emitted as a candidate. This is intentional persistence rather than malicious behavior, so the risk is medium and publishable.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections refer to Markdown inline-code markers and a fenced JSON example, not executable Ruby or shell code. No evidence found of shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the flagged characters delimit identifiers or an example payload. There is no script file or instruction to run a shell command.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections point to descriptive metadata lines. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing routines, encryption calls, or password handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations contain plain descriptions of the ChatCrystal task writeback skill. They do not contain code or any named weak cryptographic primitive.

content.value_statement: Important implementation lessons are often lost after a coding session ends. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code save reusable task outcomes into ChatCrystal memory.
actual_capabilities: Identifies completed work that produced a durable fix, pitfall, pattern, or decision.
Uses ChatCrystal Core to decide whether a memory should be created, merged, or skipped.
Calls write_task_memory when the runtime has MCP access and a stable run key.
Emits a structured memory candidate when automatic persistence is unavailable.
Keeps saved knowledge project-scoped by default.
limitations: Requires ChatCrystal Core and MCP access for automatic writeback.
Cannot save memories when no stable run or session key is available.
Does not implement its own skip or merge logic.
May persist project metadata when users approve memory writeback.

use_cases.title: Preserve Debugging Lessons
use_cases.description: Save the root cause and resolution after a difficult bug fix so future sessions can reuse the lesson.

use_cases.title: Record Team Decisions
use_cases.description: Capture durable implementation decisions after architecture or workflow changes are completed.

use_cases.title: Build Project Memory
use_cases.description: Create reusable project-scoped memory candidates when automatic ChatCrystal writeback is not available.

prompt_templates.title: Save a Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback to save the durable lesson from this completed bug fix.

prompt_templates.title: Capture a Pitfall
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback to record the pitfall, root cause, resolution, and relevant files from this task.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Project Memory
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback after this implementation. Keep the memory project-scoped and report whether it was created, merged, or skipped.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Manual Candidate
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback in degraded mode. Emit a structured memory candidate because automatic writeback is not available.

output_examples.input: A schema validation bug was fixed by reusing a shared request shape.
output_examples.output: Created a project memory that records the schema mismatch, the shared-shape fix, and the files that should stay aligned.

output_examples.input: A task produced a useful lesson, but ChatCrystal Core was unavailable.
output_examples.output: Prepared a memory candidate for later save and clearly stated that no memory was persisted.

output_examples.input: A completed refactor did not produce reusable knowledge.
output_examples.output: Skipped memory creation and reported that there was no durable lesson to save.

best_practices: Use the skill only after substantive work is complete.
Review paths, branch names, and file lists before saving sensitive project memories.
Keep memories concise, durable, and focused on reusable lessons.
anti_patterns: Do not save routine progress updates as durable memories.
Do not claim that a memory was saved when only a candidate was emitted.
Do not use global scope unless the knowledge is clearly cross-project and manually approved.

faq.question: What does this skill save?
faq.answer: It saves durable task outcomes such as fixes, pitfalls, reusable patterns, and engineering decisions.

faq.question: Does it require ChatCrystal Core?
faq.answer: Yes. Automatic writeback requires ChatCrystal Core, MCP access, and a stable run or session key.

faq.question: What happens without automatic writeback?
faq.answer: The skill emits a structured memory candidate and states that no memory was saved.

faq.question: Can it save sensitive project details?
faq.answer: Yes. It can include paths, branch names, files touched, and error signatures, so users should review sensitive context.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found of shell command execution. The static hits are Markdown formatting false positives.

faq.question: Should every task create a memory?
faq.answer: No. The skill should skip tasks that do not produce reusable knowledge.
